evals.name: clear-readme-todo-api
evals.prompt: I just cloned this repo into the current directory and I honestly have no idea what it is. Can you get me up to speed on what it does, and then tell me what you could help me with?
evals.expected_output: Recon of the repo; deduced purpose = an Express REST API for managing todo lists; writes .claude/notes/repo-onboard.md; asks the user to confirm the purpose; then asks what they want done. Does NOT start coding.
assertions: Deduces the repo is an Express REST API for managing todo lists
Anchors the deduction to concrete evidence (express dep / src/server.js routes / README)
Writes the deduced understanding to .claude/notes/repo-onboard.md
Explicitly asks the user to confirm or correct the deduced purpose
Asks the user what they would like done next instead of assuming a task
Does not start modifying code or begin a task before the user chooses

evals.name: no-readme-infer-cli
evals.prompt: what does this project actually do? walk me through it and let me know where i should start
evals.expected_output: There is no README, so purpose must be inferred from setup.py console_scripts + cli.py: a Python CLI that converts CSV files to JSON. Writes the note, confirms purpose with user, asks what to do. Should not hallucinate a purpose beyond the evidence.
assertions: Infers, with no README present, that it is a command-line tool converting CSV files to JSON
Grounds the inference in the entry point (setup.py console_scripts csv2json / cli.py argparse)
Does not invent functionality beyond what the code shows (no hallucinated features)
Writes the deduced understanding to .claude/notes/repo-onboard.md
Explicitly asks the user to confirm or correct the deduced purpose
Asks the user what they would like done next instead of assuming a task
Does not start modifying code or begin a task before the user chooses

evals.name: readme-contradicts-code
evals.prompt: onboard me to this codebase please
evals.expected_output: README claims it's a real-time analytics dashboard, but package.json (main/module/types/files=dist, peerDeps react, no start script, publishConfig) + src exporting Button/Card/Chart show it's a publishable React component library. The skill should surface this contradiction rather than parroting the README, confirm with the user, and ask what they want.
assertions: Concludes the repo is a publishable React UI component library, not a running dashboard/app
Explicitly flags the README (claims real-time analytics dashboard) vs code (dist build, react peerDeps, exports Button/Card/Chart, no app entry) contradiction
Does not blindly parrot the README's dashboard description as the truth
Writes the deduced understanding to .claude/notes/repo-onboard.md
Explicitly asks the user to confirm or correct the deduced purpose
Asks the user what they would like done next instead of assuming a task
Does not start modifying code or begin a task before the user chooses
